briefing.headline: Rig up a Carolina Rig — aggressive pre-spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for pre-spawn with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag devoid of deadstick pauses.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag d
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Carolina rig Senko produces best in prespawn and postspawn when bass stage in front of spawning banks 6-10ft deep. Use 1oz tungsten weight with 4ft leader of 12lb fluorocarbon
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: At Santee Cooper, pre-spawn keys were 10 ft and shallower, hard places and rock (hard bottom, rock, gravel), plus brush and stumps. Better if you had both rock and wood but didn't need both

KVD: In cold/post-frontal pre-spawn, don't just wind the Thunder Cricket. Work it like a jig — hop, twitch, pull-and-stop. Get it down in the grass and on the bottom. Bites are extremely subtle: the bait just gets heavy or the vibration stops. 'I ...

Palaniuk: 'I call it floating a crankbait -- pick a crankbait that dives deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel it so it crawls up and over rocks, then back down the other side. It looks like a crawfish slowly scooting.'
Robertson: In Florida grass (pencil reeds, flat reeds, Kissimmee grass, hyacinth), drop the trolling motor and cover water with swimbaits rather than sitting on one spot.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
